# turbine-api-docs

> Community-maintained reference for the Turbine Alpha HTTP API by
> PropellerHeads. Reverse-engineered from the public
> `app.turbine.exchange` source map and verified against production
> on 2026-04-13. Not affiliated with PropellerHeads.

Turbine is an intent-based DEX ("dark solver") by PropellerHeads.
Users sign off-chain `OrderIntent` messages + Permit2 typed data;
a TEE-resident solver matches them and an operator-funded executor
EOA settles them on-chain. No official public API documentation
existed when this reference was written — the team lists "Turbine
Docs" as "soon" on their site.

## Quick facts

- Base URL: `https://api.turbine.exchange/api`
- Chain: Ethereum mainnet (chainId 1)
- Auth: SIWE (EIP-4361), `personal_sign`, cookie session
- Order signing: Permit2 `PermitSingle` EIP-712 (the `OrderIntent`
  itself is NOT signed)
- Minimum order value: **$30 USD**, enforced server-side
- Platform fee: **~0.99 bps** (0.0099%) flat of mid-price notional,
  deducted from order creator's realized spread
- BigInts in responses: hex-prefixed strings ("0x1a")
- `yParity` in signatures: **bool for `/add_order`**, **hex string
  for `/verify`** (two different Rust structs server-side)
- Status enum seen: `"Active"` (TitleCase) for resting orders

## Docs

- [README](README.md): quick reference + pinned contract addresses
- [Overview](docs/overview.md): architecture, executor model, Alpha caveats
- [Authentication](docs/authentication.md): SIWE flow details
- [API reference](docs/api-reference.md): every endpoint with payloads
- [Orders](docs/orders.md): `OrderIntent` fields, lifecycle, min size
- [Permit2](docs/permit2.md): ERC-20 approval + `PermitSingle` signing
- [Wire format](docs/wire-format.md): hex bigints, yParity dual convention
- [Fees](docs/fees.md): platform fee measurement + impact on MM yield
- [Integration guide](docs/integration-guide.md): pragmatic client-building notes

## Examples

- [minimal_client.py](examples/minimal_client.py): self-contained Python reference implementing status check, SIWE auth, Permit2 signing, `/add_order`, `/order_states`, `/cancel_order`

## Full plain-text dump

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): all docs concatenated into a single plain-text file, suitable for feeding to an LLM context window whole.
